description: §6.2 Shape-4 const-derived carve-out (ARRAY form): a DERIVED cell with an ARRAY type annotation and no RHS (`const <items>: string[]`) is a derived-with-no-expression error — E-DECL-NEEDS-INITIALIZER — exactly like the scalar form. The §6.2 closing parenthetical (SPEC.md:2213) is UNCONDITIONAL over `T`: there is NO array carve-out. Shape 4's no-RHS canonical-empty default (`T[]`→`[]`) applies to PLAIN reactive cells only; a derived (`const`) cell's value IS its expression, so omitting the RHS leaves nothing to derive `[]` from. Companion to the scalar `reactive/decl-needs-initializer-pos`; pins the S260 impl fix that removed the former array exemption (was `isConst && !isArrayType`, ast-builder.js — `const`-typed arrays used to silently synthesize `[]`). Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 / E-DG-002 / E-SCOPE-001 ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §6.2 Shape 4, closing parenthetical (SPEC.md:2213): "A `const`-derived cell still requires an expression — `const <x>: T` with no RHS is a derived-with-no-expression error, NOT covered by this shape." `T` is unqualified — `const <items>: string[]` is a `const`-derived cell with no expression and therefore an error. The Shape-4 table (int→0, string→"", bool→false, `T[]`→[]) governs plain reactive cells; a derived cell's value IS its expression, so omitting the RHS leaves nothing to recompute from.
